---
# Source Card: Faltering growth: recognition and management of faltering growth in children (NICE guideline NG75)

## Summary
NICE guideline NG75 (published 27 September 2017) defines chart-relative "thresholds for concern" for recognising faltering growth in infants and children, expressed in UK-WHO growth-chart centile spaces and centile lines. It provides birthweight-stratified weight-centile-fall triggers, a current-weight cutoff (below the 2nd centile), a BMI cutoff for children over 2 years (below the 0.4th centile), and a length/height-vs-mid-parental-centile rule. For the pediatric CDS module these are population- and chart-conditioned assessment triggers (a proposal for the CDS converter), not diagnoses, treatments, or dosing directives.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) Values are framed as thresholds for concern (triggers), not diagnoses — locator: Rec 1.2.1 intro — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:41c8092c3d63611f02b67065c6d5a3138326b4733618925c5b29547531b5ec56]"
- (ev_002) Birthweight <9th centile: fall across >=1 weight centile space — locator: Rec 1.2.1 bullet — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:1168523f9864b7d8dcafdba49a438b15bbc49bf5dadcb5c0faedcd0044996369]"
- (ev_003) Birthweight 9th-91st centiles: fall across >=2 weight centile spaces — locator: Rec 1.2.1 bullet — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:9acb4c013b0a97d6c6cb45d3f0d75ee58b74b69e224e5b323ac0a76f8e90f81e]"
- (ev_004) Birthweight >91st centile: fall across >=3 weight centile spaces — locator: Rec 1.2.1 bullet — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:28977897b1daa52403a5223888ac6bdd54031954c7d09c3cb45e053f67ef577c]"
- (ev_005) Current weight <2nd centile for age is a concern whatever the birthweight — locator: Rec 1.2.1 bullet — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:2e28f0825a546e1ba6113c12f83de76149d322cf806c1e45f635f9c3ee1bf056]"
- (ev_006) 'Centile space' defined as space between adjacent UK-WHO centile lines — locator: Rec 1.2.1 parenthetical — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:c7b8d76960fe354301a0eabfbddff064f89f707ffae0c7da769c71fe0c728c01]"
- (ev_007) Children over 2y: BMI <0.4th centile suggests probable undernutrition — locator: Rec 1.2.4 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:4557d0a1d2fc48fc08e1f8b37ad11d1fce5e660c467204d2cc8203b5d041d977]"
- (ev_008) Length/height >2 centile spaces below mid-parental centile — locator: Rec 1.2.3 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:3f0888cc2f59cf8d67c26f6de8b328f446297090c4912a393bfe7cd1f0692da7]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: infants and children on UK-WHO growth charts; several thresholds are conditioned on birthweight band (below 9th / 9th-91st / above 91st centile) and age (BMI rule applies to children over 2 years).
- Assay/method: none of these are laboratory analyzer/assay-dependent; they are anthropometric measurements (weight, length/height, BMI) plotted on UK-WHO growth charts. Correct chart selection (sex-specific UK-WHO) and correctly plotted, adequately spaced measurements are prerequisites.
- Lifecycle: guideline NG75, effective/published 2017-09-27; no retirement date recorded in the recommendations page as accessed.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- These are "thresholds for concern" that trigger further assessment/investigation rather than executable rules; NG75 elsewhere directs assessment and, where indicated, referral. This card records the recognition triggers only; downstream assessment/referral pathways are outside the extracted passages.

## Limitations & conflicts
- Thresholds are chart-relative: "centile space" and centile positions (2nd, 0.4th, 9th, 91st) are defined by the UK-WHO growth charts; the rules cannot be applied without the correct chart and its centile-line spacing.
- UK-WHO centile conventions differ from CDC/WHO percentile conventions used in US pediatric practice; do not silently equate a UK-WHO "2nd centile" with a CDC 2nd/3rd/5th percentile — this is a preserved cross-source convention difference, not to be collapsed.
- "Last updated" date was not stated on the recommendations page as accessed; version recorded as original 2017-09-27 publication.
- No numeric threshold in this source was asserted without a verbatim passage; all eight evidence points carry an exact-passage quote.
